Mirrors  >  Home  >  MPE Home  >  Th. List  >  ssiun Structured version   Visualization version   GIF version
Theorem ssiun 5004
Description: Subset implication for an indexed union. (Contributed by NM, 3-Sep-2003.) (Proof shortened by Andrew Salmon, 25-Jul-2011.)
Assertion
Ref Expression
ssiun ⊢ (∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐶 ⊆ 𝐵 → 𝐶 ⊆ ∪ 𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐵)
Distinct variable group:   𝑥,𝐶
Allowed substitution hints:   𝐴(𝑥)   𝐵(𝑥)
Proof of Theorem ssiun
Dummy variable 𝑦 is distinct from all other variables.
StepHypRef Expression
1 ssel 3930 . . . . 5 ⊢ (𝐶 ⊆ 𝐵 → (𝑦 ∈ 𝐶 → 𝑦 ∈ 𝐵))
21reximi 3100 . . . 4 ⊢ (∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐶 ⊆ 𝐵 → ∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 (𝑦 ∈ 𝐶 → 𝑦 ∈ 𝐵))
3 r19.37v 3188 . . . 4 ⊢ (∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 (𝑦 ∈ 𝐶 → 𝑦 ∈ 𝐵) → (𝑦 ∈ 𝐶 → ∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝑦 ∈ 𝐵))
42, 3syl 17 . . 3 ⊢ (∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐶 ⊆ 𝐵 → (𝑦 ∈ 𝐶 → ∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝑦 ∈ 𝐵))
5 eliun 4953 . . 3 ⊢ (𝑦 ∈ ∪ 𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐵 ↔ ∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝑦 ∈ 𝐵)
64, 5imbitrrdi 254 . 2 ⊢ (∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐶 ⊆ 𝐵 → (𝑦 ∈ 𝐶 → 𝑦 ∈ ∪ 𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐵))
76ssrdv 3942 1 ⊢ (∃𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐶 ⊆ 𝐵 → 𝐶 ⊆ ∪ 𝑥 ∈ 𝐴 𝐵)
Colors of variables: wff setvar class
Syntax hints:   → wi 4   ∈ wcel 2142  ∃wrex 3086   ⊆ wss 3904  ∪ ciun 4949
